source_depth_status: final evidence-count target met; needs human expert review

sources.title: Cerebral Microbleeds: A Review of Clinical, Genetic, and Neuroimaging Associations
sources.notes: Primary broad source for microbleed definition, common MRI appearance, etiologic contexts, associated SVD markers, and risk framing.

sources.title: Neuroimaging standards for research into small vessel disease and its contribution to ageing and neurodegeneration
sources.notes: STRIVE v1 consensus source for standard terminology and reporting of vascular changes on neuroimaging.

sources.title: Neuroimaging standards for research into small vessel disease-advances since 2013
sources.notes: Use for updated STRIVE context and evolving research standards; not yet deeply extracted.

sources.title: MR Imaging Detection of Cerebral Microbleeds: Effect of Susceptibility-Weighted Imaging, Section Thickness, and Field Strength
sources.notes: Use for acquisition/protocol confounders; do not use as broad clinical natural-history source.

sources.title: Determination of detection sensitivity for cerebral microbleeds using susceptibility weighted imaging
sources.notes: Use for technical sensitivity and sequence-parameter caveats.

sources.title: SWI or T2*: Which MRI Sequence to Use in the Detection of Cerebral Microbleeds? The Karolinska Imaging Dementia Study
sources.notes: Use for practical sequence comparison and research comparability warnings.

sources.title: Automated detection of cerebral microbleeds on T2*-weighted MRI
sources.notes: Useful for research-method and rating/automation sections, plus figure evidence of SWI/GRE microbleed appearance.

sources.title: The Microbleed Anatomical Rating Scale (MARS): reliability of a tool to map brain microbleeds
sources.notes: Use for structured count/location rating and research reproducibility.

sources.title: Cerebral Microbleeds: Imaging and Clinical Significance
sources.notes: High-authority radiology review; extract more detail in later maturation cycle.

sources.title: Cerebral microbleeds: from depiction to interpretation
sources.notes: Use for clinical interpretation and uncertainty around management decisions.

sources.title: Intracerebral hemorrhage and small vessel disease
sources.notes: Use for macrohemorrhage context and small-vessel disease marker grouping.

sources.title: Diagnosis of Cerebral Amyloid Angiopathy: Evolution of the Boston Criteria
sources.notes: Use for CAA-related lobar microbleed context and Boston criteria evolution.

sources.title: Neuroradiological Findings in Cerebral Amyloid Angiopathy with a Particular Consideration of the Boston Criteria 2.0
sources.notes: Use for CAA/Boston 2.0 imaging marker framing and mimic exclusions.

sources.title: Cerebral Amyloid Angiopathy - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf
sources.notes: Used only for CAA context; microbleed chapter should not become a CAA-only chapter.

sources.title: Cerebral amyloid angiopathy - Radiopaedia
sections_supported: mimics including hypertensive microangiopathy and cavernomas
sources.notes: Use for radiology-facing differential language and CAA-related microhemorrhage mimics.

sources.title: Hypertensive microangiopathy - Radiopaedia
sources.notes: Use for deep gray/brainstem microbleed distribution contrast with CAA-like lobar distribution.

sources.title: Cerebral Microbleeds (CMB) - Stroke Manual
sources.notes: Recent educational clinical reference; use as supportive, not primary guideline evidence.

sources.title: Susceptibility weighted imaging: Clinical applications and future directions
sources.notes: Use for SWI-sensitive blood-product detection and distribution examples.

sources.title: Spontaneous brain microbleeds: systematic review, subgroup analyses and standards for study design and reporting
sources.notes: Useful historical standards source for study-design problems: MRI parameter heterogeneity, size definition variation, bias, and reporting standards.

sources.title: Cerebral microbleeds: a guide to detection and interpretation
sources.notes: Seminal Microbleed Study Group review for how detection depends on pulse sequence, field strength, spatial resolution, and post-processing.

sources.title: Cerebral microbleeds a guide to detection and interpretation
sources.notes: Open institutional record for the Lancet Neurology guide; used when publisher page is abstract-only.

sources.title: Improving interrater agreement about brain microbleeds: development of the Brain Observer MicroBleed Scale (BOMBS)
sources.notes: Adds BOMBS rating scale evidence for standardizing microbleed number and distribution assessment.

sources.title: Cerebral microbleed detection and mapping: Principles, methodological aspects and rationale in vascular dementia
sources.notes: Review focused on practical microbleed identification, mimics, standardized scales, and emerging automated detection.

sources.title: Microbleeds in vascular dementia: clinical aspects
sources.notes: Supports vascular cognitive impairment context and limits claims about clinical meaning.

sources.title: How to assess the reliability of cerebral microbleed rating?
sources.notes: Use for quality-control language around rater agreement, adjudication, and reliability metrics.

sources.title: Cerebral Microbleeds: Burden Assessment by Using Quantitative Susceptibility Mapping
sources.notes: Adds QSM-specific evidence for reducing inconsistency in microbleed burden measurement.

sources.title: Semi-Automated Detection of Cerebral Microbleeds on 3.0 T MR Images
sources.notes: Supports research-method discussion for semiautomated detection and rater burden in large studies.

sources.title: Automated characterisation of cerebral microbleeds using their size and spatial distribution on brain MRI
sources.notes: Adds modern evidence for automated size/location metrics and MARS-region atlas use.

sources.title: Prevalence and risk factors of cerebral microbleeds: The Rotterdam Scan Study
sources.notes: Supports population prevalence and location-specific etiologic hypotheses.

sources.title: The Rotterdam Scan Study: design and update up to 2012
sources.notes: Useful for why sequence sensitivity changes prevalence and burden estimates in population imaging.

sources.title: Clinical significance of cerebral microbleeds on MRI: A comprehensive meta-analysis of risk of intracerebral hemorrhage, ischemic stroke, mortality, and dementia in cohort studies
sources.notes: Broad outcome source for risk framing across cohorts; avoid using as patient-level prognosis.

sources.title: Cerebral microbleeds and stroke risk after ischaemic stroke or transient ischaemic attack: pooled individual patient data
sources.notes: High-value source for separating relative and absolute risk in ischemic stroke/TIA cohorts.

sources.title: Antiplatelet Treatment After Transient Ischemic Attack and Ischemic Stroke in Patients With Cerebral Microbleeds
sources.notes: Supports treatment-temporal confounding and careful endpoint framing in antiplatelet-treated stroke/TIA cohorts.

sources.title: Cerebral microbleeds and the risk of intracerebral haemorrhage after thrombolysis for acute ischaemic stroke
sources.notes: DARE/NCBI summary of systematic review for prethrombolysis microbleed risk context.

sources.title: Postthrombolysis intracranial hemorrhage risk of cerebral microbleeds in acute stroke patients
sources.notes: Second DARE summary to capture postthrombolysis hemorrhage-risk literature and its limits.

sources.title: Clinical Outcome in Acute Ischemic Stroke Patients With Microbleeds After Thrombolytic Therapy: A Meta-Analysis
sources.notes: Use for outcome and treatment confounding, not for patient-level therapy advice.

sources.title: Diffuse axonal injury after traumatic cerebral microbleeds: an evaluation of imaging techniques
sources.notes: Adds trauma/DAI mimic context and SWI detection evidence.

sources.title: Cortical Localization of Microbleeds in Cerebral Amyloid Angiopathy: An Ultra High-Field 7T MRI Study
sources.notes: Supports CAA-related cortical/lobar localization but should stay scoped to ultra-high-field context.

sources.title: Heterogeneous histopathology of cortical microbleeds in cerebral amyloid angiopathy
sources.notes: Adds important caution that MRI-defined microbleeds can have heterogeneous histopathologic substrates.

sources.title: Microbleed and microinfarct detection in amyloid angiopathy: a high-resolution MRI-histopathology study
sources.notes: Supports source-grounded claim boundaries when connecting imaging markers to pathology.

sources.title: Cortical superficial siderosis progression in cerebral amyloid angiopathy: Prospective MRI study
sources.notes: Useful for related hemorrhagic marker context and serial imaging change in CAA.

sources.title: Cortical superficial siderosis: detection and clinical significance in cerebral amyloid angiopathy and related conditions
sources.notes: Adds related superficial siderosis marker context; not a microbleed-only source.

sources.title: Amyloid-related Imaging Abnormalities (ARIA) in the Context of Alzheimer's Disease and Amyloid-targeting Therapies
sources.notes: Adds treatment-related ARIA-H terminology, severity bins, and monitoring context.

sources.title: Amyloid-related imaging abnormalities (ARIA): diagnosis, management, and care in the setting of amyloid-modifying therapy
sources.notes: Use for ARIA-H severity language and treatment-temporal confounding; avoid patient-level recommendations.

sources.title: Anti-Amyloid Therapy, AD, and ARIA: Untangling the Role of CAA
sources.notes: Adds CAA/ARIA link and microhemorrhage severity context for anti-amyloid therapy studies.

sources.title: A regional framework for the detection and management of ARIA with anti-amyloid therapies in early Alzheimer's disease in Asia
sources.notes: Very recent regional ARIA-H framework; useful for monitoring vocabulary and severity definitions.

sources.title: Practical Brain MRI Guidelines for Anti-Aβ Antibody Treatment in Early Symptomatic Alzheimer's Disease
sources.notes: Adds practical MRI checklist language for ARIA-H, differential diagnoses, and protocol considerations.

sources.title: Differences in lobar microbleed topography in cerebral amyloid angiopathy and hypertensive arteriopathy
sources.notes: Use for nuanced lobar microbleed topography; not a standalone diagnostic rule.

sources.title: MRI-visible perivascular spaces in cerebral amyloid angiopathy and hypertensive arteriopathy
sources.notes: Adds related small-vessel-disease marker context for differential and covariate design.

sources.title: Lobar Distribution of Cerebral Microbleeds: The Rotterdam Scan Study
sources.notes: Adds population-based lobar distribution detail beyond simple lobar/deep grouping.
